perturbation.original_text: Each such invoice shall reflect only those costs that have been incurred in performance of the RESEARCH PROJECT and shall provide a breakdown of costs similar to the detail set forth in the budget of the related SCHEDULE. CERES will pay all such invoices within thirty (30) days after the invoice date.
perturbation.changed_text: Each such invoice shall reflect only those costs that have been incurred in performance of the RESEARCH PROJECT and shall provide a breakdown of costs similar to the detail set forth in the budget of the related SCHEDULE. CERES will pay all such invoices within sixty (60) days after the invoice date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment deadline for invoices from thirty (30) days to sixty (60) days, while the previous sentence stipulates it must be thirty (30) days creating a conflict in the expected enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Where a SCHEDULE provides that CERES will make a financial contribution to certain RESEARCH PROJECT activities performed by IGER, unless agreed otherwise in the appropriate SCHEDULE, IGER will submit invoices after the end of each calendar quarter for the work performed during that quarter. Each such invoice shall reflect only those costs that have been incurred in performance of the RESEARCH PROJECT and shall provide a breakdown of costs similar to the detail set forth in the budget of the related SCHEDULE. CERES will pay all such invoices within sixty (60) days after the invoice date. Payments shall be remitted to: Institute Secretary Institute of Grassland and Environmental Research Plas Gogerddan Aberystwyth Ceredigion SY23 3EB United Kingdom
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated payment within thirty (30) days, and the changed text altered this to sixty (60) days. The contradicted text includes the same payment clause of sixty (60) days in another schedule. Although the contradiction is now between the original text and the changed text, before both sections agreed on thirty (30) days, now they differ creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'. This introduces a clear conflict regarding the payment timeline. The changed text, 'CERES will pay all such invoices within sixty (60) days after the invoice date' contradicts the original implicit agreement of 'CERES will pay all such invoices within thirty (30) days after the invoice date' that was previously present across both sections.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 The non-disclosing Party shall have the thirty (30) day notice period to object, in writing, to such proposed Disclosure because it contains (a) potentially patentable subject matter that needs protection or (b) BACKGROUND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY or JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY or INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY of the non-disclosing Party or OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS which need to be kept confidential for compelling business reasons in the non-disclosing Party's reasonable judgment.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 The non-disclosing Party shall have the sixty (60) day notice period to object, in writing, to such proposed Disclosure because it contains (a) potentially patentable subject matter that needs protection or (b) BACKGROUND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY or JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY or INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY of the non-disclosing Party or OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS which need to be kept confidential for compelling business reasons in the non-disclosing Party's reasonable judgment.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the notification period from thirty (30) to sixty (60) days, creates a contradiction within the Article regarding how long the non-disclosing party has to object.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The non-disclosing Party shall have the sixty (60) day notice period to object, in writing, to such proposed Disclosure because it contains (a) potentially patentable subject matter that needs protection or (b) BACKGROUND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY or JOINT INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY or INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY of the non-disclosing Party or OTHER RESEARCH RESULTS which need to be kept confidential for compelling business reasons in the non-disclosing Party's reasonable judgment. In the event that the non-disclosing Party makes such objection, the disclosing Party will (x) if applicable, elect to cooperate with the non-disclosing Party to obtain proper protection in accordance with the provisions of Article 4 herein or (y) remove the objectionable subject matter from the Disclosure. No delay caused by the non-disclosing Party under this Article shall extend beyond three (3) months, wherein upon such delay, the disclosing Party shall be permitted to proceed without being in breach of this Article.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a thirty (30) day notice period, while the changed text modifies this to a sixty (60) day notice period. The contradicted text also specifies a sixty (60) day notice period. This creates a direct in-text contradiction, as the original thirty (30) day period is inconsistent with the sixty (60) day period mentioned elsewhere in the document, specifically in the contradicted text.
